rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as core prior evidence on price elasticity levels and trends, not just as a background list citation. The citing paper draws directly on Bijmolt et al. (2005) to motivate hypotheses (e.g., increasing price sensitivity over time), to position expected magnitudes relative to advertising elasticities, and to benchmark its own estimated elasticities and time-trend results. It is also cited in the methodological framing as identifying confounds in meta-analytic evidence that the current single-source design seeks to avoid. These roles indicate a meaningful framing and interpretive dependence.
relation_description: The citing study uses Bijmolt et al. (2005) as an empirical foundation for expected price-elasticity magnitude and trend, and for motivating hypotheses about growing consumer price sensitivity. It then compares its own elasticity estimates and trend rates against that prior meta-analytic benchmark.
